capabilities: Combine event sourcing with SQL read models to enhance data retrieval efficiency and consistency by leveraging agent execution insights.

procedure.action: Reconcile artifacts from event sourcing and agent execution by applying field-level structural comparison on shared key fields; flag conflicts where hash(A.field) ≠ hash(B.field) and log to conflict_log.
Assert merged output for consistency by asserting all required fields pass JSON Schema draft-07 validation; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field.
Filter candidate events for read model updates by executing SQL queries that identify changes in event streams; ensure query execution time is logged and below 100ms.
Assign results from event streams using domain-specific classification algorithms; ensure classification accuracy is logged and exceeds 95%.

Log operation duration and result code for each step to an audit trail using structured logging; ensure logs are accessible for compliance and debugging purposes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Optimize SQL read models by integrating event sourcing insights with agent execution knowledge, ensuring efficient data retrieval and consistency.
title: Optimizing SQL Read Models for Event Sourcing

failureConditions: Inconsistent data when merge omits NULL handling: records with NULL key fields are silently dropped, causing referential integrity violations in downstream consumers.
High latency in read models when SQL queries are not optimized for event stream changes, causing delayed data retrieval.
Incorrect classification results when domain-specific algorithms are not properly tuned, leading to inaccurate data insights.
metrics: merged output for consistency by asserting all required fields pass JSON Schema draft-07 validation
